title: hormuz-open-or-closed — 2026-05-10
narrative: The White House and US Central Command have publicly declared the Strait of Hormuz "open for business" under Operation Freedom, claiming the US naval blockade has successfully restored safe passage for international shipping. President Trump stated ships are "flowing freely" and cited "unprecedented success" in opening the waterway. CENTCOM reports that vessels are transiting safely.
telemetry: AIS ship-tracking data (BBC Verify, NBC News) shows only 15 vessels crossed in the first days of the "open" period — against a historical baseline of 138/day
9 of those 15 vessels had direct links to Iran (Iranian-flagged, Iran-port departures)
No tanker carrying a full oil/gas load was documented exiting the Gulf during this period
Lloyd's List reports ~800 ships remain stuck inside the Gulf for weeks
Ships that ARE transiting are taking a northerly route hugging Iran's coastline — inside IRGC permitted corridors — rather than the normal central shipping lane
US Navy is actively clearing sea mines laid by Iran's IRGC, contradicting the "safe passage" narrative
GPS spoofing is documented; exact vessel counts are unknowable
Gas prices hit $4+/gallon in the US (40% March spike), contradicting official "operations successful" framing
analysis:
Official sources say one thing. The evidence says another. The Strait is not open. The 15-vessel trickle — 9 Iran-linked, hugging Iran's shoreline, with active minesweeping operations ongoing — is a managed corridor, not a restored waterway. The ~800 ships stuck inside represent a dam that hasn't broken. This is a managed narrative for domestic political consumption while the economic pain compounds.
